mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or out-of-sample t-statistic for return difference falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving higher corporate revenue expectations and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample regression testing over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term equity momentum through improved profit margins.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction and predicts negative returns during high-inflation regimes, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in mid-cycle samples.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index reflects rising inflation expectations, prompting market participants to reprice future corporate cash flows at higher discount rates and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 60 percent during CPI acceleration periods, or predictive accuracy fails to exceed random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates economic stagnation, reducing corporate profit forecasts and triggering negative equity returns as investors discount future earnings.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of low-growth observations, or signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting equity valuations through higher earnings expectations and wage-driven consumption.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 2.0 percent during inflationary periods triggers monetary tightening fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative short-term returns as borrowing costs rise.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation is below 2 percent, or positive returns dominate in more than 55 percent of triggered cases.
